message:
Record frankenfs bd-nt3t fuzz pipeline expansion task in beads

Persist a new high-priority beads issue into the JSONL audit log so
`br ready` and `bv --robot-triage` surface it on their next pass
without waiting for whoever happens to manually flush later.

Issue details captured:

- id: bd-nt3t
- title: "Expand fuzz pipeline to cover all registered cargo-fuzz
  targets"
- type: task, priority 1 (P1 / high)
- status: in_progress (created_at and updated_at are ~5 seconds apart,
  so the claim happened in the same working session that opened the
  ticket)
- labels: ["fuzzing", "testing"]
- scope recorded in the description: align nightly campaigns,
  crash-promotion infrastructure, corpus expectations, and acceptance
  checks with the actual fuzz target manifest in `fuzz/Cargo.toml`;
  add the missing target coverage and verify via remote build/test.

No code, fuzz fixtures, or CI config change in this commit — only the
tracked `.beads/issues.jsonl` ledger moves so the audit history stays
faithful to the live DB. As with every prior beads commit in frankenfs,
the file is force-added because `.beads/` is gitignored but
`issues.jsonl` itself is tracked.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
